author: PC Gamer
text:
Jason Momoa drops out of the Helldivers movie
Sony is now looking for a new star.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Once again players are right to suspect AI was used in a game, once again a dev apologizes for using AI in their game
1666: Amsterdam dropped a cool trailer at Summer Game Fest, and you know what happened next.

author: PC Gamer
text:
'An evolved game of cat and mouse:' Alien: Isolation 2 leads on taking the alien outside, the good kind of AI, and why a sequel took so long
The horror sequel's creative director and lead designer on what it's like to face the Alien outdoors.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Are these real posts from the r/skyrimmods subreddit, or did I make them up? Take our newest and rudest quiz!
Truth is stranger than fiction in our new quiz.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Ubisoft closes another 2 studios, lays off hundreds more employees
Ubisoft Winnipeg and Belgrade are gone, and nearly a third of its studio in Barcelona have been let go.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Ico and Shadow of the Colossus creator Fumito Ueda would like to see his old games on PC someday, too
Ueda's next game, Gen Atlas, is the first on PC in a 25 year directorial career.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Xbox 'console exclusives' are strictly about console releases, Xbox exec affirms: Games will 'still show up on all the normal places where we sell the PC version'
Some much needed clarity from Matt Booty.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Steam Frame's tutorial video appears to leak on Reddit, demonstrating a very brief explainer of its software
Here's hoping we actually see it release soon.

author: PC Gamer
text:
18 embargoes from Summer Game Fest just lifted, so let's rank everything I saw in LA
A gauntlet of new games.

author: PC Gamer
text:
All 26 new catalysts in Destiny 2's final update
How to get them and what they do.

author: PC Gamer
text:
After a 'corporate mandate' demanded they help with Call of Duty, Toys for Bob studio head says it took 'many mini miracles' to buy their independence from Activision
"We knew that it wasn't the right fit."

author: PC Gamer
text:
Logitech's new mouse comes with a little bag to carry it around in. Oh, and it's foldable too
Accessorise, accessorise, accessorise.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Old Persona 4 voice actor is 'deeply grieving' being recast in the remake: 'Everything in life is transient… Thank you for sharing the journey'
This was not our chance.

author: PC Gamer
text:
The Fable reboot won't have horns and haloes because of its 'nuanced and complex' take on morality, and also because you're going to constantly reinvent yourself
It's like uni all over again.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Redditor finds AMD museum piece that once belonged to ex-CEO Rory Read, stashed away in a basement
Signed and sealed.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Archetype Entertainment founder and BioWare veteran wishes Exodus didn't look so much like Mass Effect
But he thinks the team is doing a "good job" moving it further away from BioWare's sci-fi romp.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Backrooms director's dad returns to old Facebook post asking for career help for his son as the movie hits #1 worldwide: 'Thanks for the help. He figured it out!'
Everyone deserves a victory lap.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Linux driver for vintage AMD GPUs gets an update with a helping hand from Copilot AI, keeping old hardware alive and kicking
Today, just refactoring. Tomorrow, the whole driver set?

author: PC Gamer
text:
Valve will stop selling Steam gift cards at retailers over scam concerns
But my CEO needed that $100 Steam gift card!

author: PC Gamer
text:
Pathfinder creators Paizo have $10 million of inventory stuck in a warehouse somewhere because of Diamond Comics, forcing it to lay off staff
"Paizo and dozens of other publishers have been in court ever since, fighting to reclaim our own products."

author: PC Gamer
text:
Google claims most users know 'information generated with AI should not be blindly trusted,' but a court ruled it's still liable for false claims made in AI Overview
I doubt this will be the last time an AI getting it wrong results in legal repercussions.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Microsoft rolls out Windows Low Latency Profile, giving older PCs a bit more snap when opening the Start Menu and apps
Every little helps, even if it really is very little.

author: PC Gamer
text:
How to complete the Distortions quest in Destiny 2
Follow the Strange Signal to get some powerful new guns.

author: PC Gamer
text:
How to score a goal in the hidden net in Destiny 2
Complete the Goal! Monument of Triumph challenge.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Both lawyers in case use hallucinating AI, causing judge to throw up hands, bar them for 2 years, fine everybody, and call the whole thing off for 60 days
I believe the legal term is "absolute malarkey".

author: PC Gamer
text:
The Mortal Shell 2 open beta was downloaded 250,000 times over the weekend
The action RPG's developer says the response has been "incredible," and it has begun fixing bugs and "tightening the experience."

author: PC Gamer
text:
Legendary JRPG studio's 23-year shun of the PC has ended: the next Vanillaware game will come to Steam
Vanillaware is getting with the times.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Donald Duck has five fingers in official art for Kingdom Hearts Collection, and that is definitely not right
Yup, it looks like we've got another one.

author: PC Gamer
text:
'The curse of MIXED is broken:' Suda51 & Swery65's Hotel Barcelona claws its way to 'Mostly Positive' on Steam after Hail Mary patch and scrubbing AI assets
Redemption arc?

author: PC Gamer
text:
Everything we know about Final Fantasy 7 Revelation, the Remake trilogy's last act
Can I defeat Emerald and Ruby Weapon as an adult? We'll know soon.
